question: Chapters
feedback: The text does not list the main chapters or sections of the thesis. To improve this, you should add a paragraph at the end of the introduction that outlines the structure of the work (e.g., 'The thesis is divided into three main parts: the first part covers..., the second part focuses on..., and the final part presents...').

question: Description
feedback: The text does not describe the contents of the thesis chapters. While it describes the problem and the relevance of the topic, it fails to explain what will be covered in each part of the research. You should briefly summarize what each chapter will entail to guide the reader through your research process.

question: Structure
feedback: The general structure of the thesis cannot be understood from this text because the 'Plan of the thesis' or 'Structure of the work' section is missing. The text focuses entirely on the problem statement and relevance. To fix this, include a concluding section in the introduction that maps out the logical flow of your chapters.
